findings.message: New `redis-exporter` service exposes Redis metrics on :9121 but `prometheus.yml` has no matching scrape job (existing jobs: app, prometheus, otel-collector, postgres). The exporter is deployed but Prometheus never scrapes it, so no Redis metrics are ever collected and this prod surface is unobserved.
findings.suggestion:
Add a scrape job to infra/observability/prometheus/prometheus.yml, e.g.
  - job_name: redis
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["redis-exporter:9121"]

findings.message: The new redis-exporter has no corresponding alert rules. infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/ contains app/otel/postgres/prometheus/slo/alertmanager rules but nothing for Redis (e.g. RedisDown, high memory/eviction). The postgres-exporter ships with postgres_alerts.yml; the redis exporter should follow the same pattern.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with at least a RedisDown alert keyed on the new scrape target (up{job="redis"} == 0).

findings.message: The new redis-exporter has no Grafana dashboard. infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ has otel-collector/postgres/prometheus/slo dashboards but no redis panel. The established pattern (postgres-exporter -> postgres.json) is not followed, so Redis metrics would be uncharted.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json with core Redis panels (connections, memory, hit rate, evictions) sourced from the redis_exporter metrics.
